experimental_data_summary.key_findings: Stereomicroscope with image analysis successfully measured wetting, gel formation, and erosion rates. Higher effervescent content increased wetting and erosion rates but decreased gel formation. Dissolution rate positively correlated with wetting rate (R²=0.9641) and erosion rate up to 0.16 mm/min. Disintegration time negatively correlated with wetting and erosion rates.
experimental_data_summary.statistical_analysis: Linear regression for correlations; model selection criteria (R², AIC, MSC) for release kinetics.
experimental_data_summary.zero_first_order_release: Not directly reported; Peppas-Sahlin model used (diffusion/erosion combined).

100_Stereomicroscope-with-Imaging-Analys.notes: The requested batch naming (1P,2P,3P,1C,2C,3C,1H,2H,3H) does not appear in the article. The actual formulation codes are IBU, IP, IP5E, IP10E, IP20E, IP25E, IP30E. All data above correspond to these. Pre-compression parameters, hardness, friability, thickness, weight variation, density, drug content, consolidation index, FTIR, lag time, floating time, swelling index were not reported in this study. Cumulative release vs time values are approximated from Figure 7 in the original article.

materials_origin.ornidazole: Gift sample from Aristo Pharmaceuticals, Daman, India (98-100.2% pure)
materials_origin.mebendazole: Gift sample from CIPLA, Bangalore, India (internal standard)
materials_origin.guar_gum: Dabar Research Foundation, New Delhi, India; USP/NF quality; viscosity of 1% aqueous dispersion is 125 cps at 25°C
materials_origin.rat_caecal_contents: Male albino rats (105-115 g) from Ghosh Enterprises, Kolkata, India; maintained on normal diet

experimental_design_for_optimization.approach: Comparison of three compression-coated formulations with varying guar gum content in coat (65%, 75%, 85%) to minimize drug release in stomach/small intestine and maximize release in colon
experimental_design_for_optimization.independent_variable: Percentage of guar gum in coat
dependent_variables: Drug release in simulated gastric fluid (0.1M HCl, 2 hr)
Drug release in simulated intestinal fluid (pH 7.4 phosphate buffer, 3 hr)
Drug release in rat caecal content medium (4% w/v, up to 24 hr)

hplc_conditions.preparation: Samples added with internal standard (mebendazole 4 μg/mL), diluted, filtered through 0.2 μm membrane
hplc_conditions.results: Linear relationship between concentration and peak area ratio (r=0.9999)
hplc_conditions.mobile_phase: Acetonitrile : water (38:62) with 0.4% triethylamine, pH adjusted to 3.6 with 0.1% orthophosphoric acid
hplc_conditions.column: RP C-18 (150 mm × 4.6 mm I.D., 5 μm particle size)

core_tablets.weight_variation: Not specified, but content uniformity performed

compression_coated_tablets.hardness: Not specified (only stated that it was determined)

independent_variables_and_levels.independent_variable: Guar gum amount in coat (mg and % of coat)

101_Studies-on-the-development-of-colon-.ftir_measurements: Not performed / not mentioned in the article

core_tablets.equipment: Single station tablet machine (Cadmach Machinery, India)
core_tablets.drug_content: Uniformity confirmed, numeric values not specified

compression_coated_tablets.equipment: Single station tablet machine (Cadmach Machinery, India)
compression_coated_tablets.drug_content: Tested, no numeric values given; no change after stability

process_parameters.compression_device: Single station tablet machine (Cadmach Machinery, India)

OLV-65.release_mechanism: Colonic bacterial degradation of guar gum coat
OLV-65.factors_influencing_release: pH (minimal release in stomach/intestine), enzymes from rat caecal contents (colonic bacteria)

OLV-75.release_mechanism: Colonic bacterial degradation of guar gum coat
OLV-75.factors_influencing_release: pH, enzymes from rat caecal contents

OLV-85.release_mechanism: Partial degradation; high guar gum content prevents complete erosion
OLV-85.factors_influencing_release: pH, insufficient enzymatic degradation due to high gel strength

compression_coated_tablets.disintegration_time: Not applicable (coat designed to remain intact in stomach/small intestine)
compression_coated_tablets.stability: No change in physical appearance, drug content, or dissolution profile after 6 months at 40°C/75% RH for OLV-65 and OLV-75 (data in Table 3)

swelling_water_uptake.lag_time: OLV-65: ~12 hr (release increase from 12 hr onward); OLV-75: ~18 hr; OLV-85: no complete degradation

statistical_analysis.results: For OLV-65 and OLV-75, significant difference (p < 0.001) between release with and without rat caecal contents at 24 hr

performance_metrics.maximal_release_in_colon: > 93% for OLV-65 and OLV-75 in presence of caecal contents
performance_metrics.stability: OLV-65 and OLV-75 stable for 6 months at 40°C/75% RH (equivalent to 2 years shelf life)

materials_origin.pectin (GENU pectin USP/100, DE 55-65%): CP Kelco ApS, Lille Skensved, Denmark
materials_origin.UPLC_grade_water: Milli-Q gradient water purification system (Millipore SAS, Molsheim, France)

preparation_of_AZT_loaded_SMMs.aqueous_phase: Ca²⁺ (0.01 M, 3.89 mL), pectin (270 mg), porcine gastric mucin (30 mg), PEG 400 (0.1 mL), AZT (300 mg)

composite_polymeric_caplet_preparation.blending: PDLL, KSR, MS, AZT-loaded SMMs (20% w/w), Carbopol 974P NF (1% w/w) blended for 5 min
composite_polymeric_caplet_preparation.compression: Direct compression on hydraulic Carver press at 2.5 tons force

formulation_details_for_each_batch.note: Only independent variables (PDLL, MS, KSR masses) are reported per batch. The masses of Carbopol 974P NF and AZT-loaded SMMs are not given per batch; they were kept at constant percentages (1% w/w and 20% w/w respectively) of the total caplet mass, but total mass varied. The table below shows the available data.

pre_compression_parameters.note: Not reported in the article. No data on mass distribution, friability, size with SD for individual batches.

tablet_fabrications_for_each_batch.note: Only hardness (N) and fractional drug release are reported per batch (see Table II). Other parameters (friability, weight variation, thickness, diameter, density, drug content, consolidation index) are not reported per batch.

process_parameters.compression_device: Hydraulic Carver press (Carver Inc., Wabash, IN, USA)
process_parameters.equipment_for_blending: Not specified (dry powder mixture blended for 5 min)
process_parameters.pressure: Equivalent to 2.5 tons force (actual pressure not calculated)

release_properties_for_each_batch.note: Individual batch release profiles not provided. Only the optimized formulation's release kinetics are detailed. The table below summarizes the optimized caplet release in two media.

media_1.name: Simulated Vaginal Fluid (SVF), pH 4.5
media_1.best_fit_models: Makoid-Banakar (R²_adj=0.997), Logistic (0.994), Probit (0.990), First order (0.990), Weibull (0.992)

media_2.name: Phosphate Buffered Saline (PBS), pH 7.4 (semen simulant)
media_2.release_mechanism: Increased swelling due to Carbopol ionization, leading to faster release

optimized_caplet.disintegration_time: Not applicable (extended release over 30 days, no disintegration)
optimized_caplet.stability: Not directly reported; X-ray imaging showed gradual erosion over 28 days in vivo

effect_of_Carbopol_content_on_swelling.1_percent_w_w: Dimensional increase: length, width, thickness (see Fig 2b; exact values not tabulated)
effect_of_Carbopol_content_on_swelling.3_percent_w_w: Greatest swelling; no significant effect of silk pouch (p<0.005)

in_vitro_results.swelling_study: Dimensional increase positively correlated with Carbopol 974P NF content; PBS caused more swelling than SVF

figure_1_approximated_release_data.description: Fractional AZT release from optimized caplet formulation in SVF (pH 4.5) and PBS (pH 7.4) over 30 days. Time points (days): 3, 7, 14, 21, 28.

in_vivo_results_pig_model.X_ray_imaging: Drug-loaded caplet showed gradual fading of contrast (erosion from outside inward); swelling observed

AZT_concentrations.plasma_range_ug_ml: 1.18 ± 0.01 to 1.13 ± 0.02 (decreased over time, Cmax on day 7)

in_vivo_results_pig_model.comparison_to_EC50_EC95: Plasma levels effective (above 102_EC50/102_EC95) but low to limit toxicity; vaginal levels higher for site-specific action
in_vivo_results_pig_model.histopathology: Scored abnormalities: epithelial erosion/ulceration (minimal to moderate), epithelial proliferation (mild to moderate), subepithelial leukocyte influx (minimal to severe), stromal fibrosis (minimal to mild), perivascular cell cuffing (minimal to moderate), tissue necrosis (isolated case, pig 102_B4). Some abnormalities attributed to device and silk pouch.

statistical_analysis.design_analysis: Box-Behnken with MINITAB; R² values: FMI 87.3%, Hardness 61.7%, FDR 77.9%
statistical_analysis.kinetic_model_fitting: Multiple models with R²_adj values ranging 0.885-0.997 (SVF) and 0.502-0.995 (PBS)

swelling_water_uptake_study.swelling_index_percent: Reported as percentage dimensional increase (length, width, thickness) for 1%,2%,3% Carbopol; exact values not tabulated but shown in Fig 2b

102_Submicron-Matrices-Embedded-in-a-Pol.missing_data_note: The following requested data were not present in the article: FTIR spectra, friability for any batch, disintegration time, weight variation, thickness/diameter per batch, density, drug content per batch, consolidation index, pre-compression parameters, individual release kinetics for 102_F1-102_F15, and detailed swelling index values. Only the optimized formulation's properties and the Box-Behnken design matrix with three responses are reported.
